intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Alaticaulia cupularis". It's possible that this plant may not be well-documented or may be a fictional species.

However, I can suggest a similar plant "Alpinia caerulea" also known as "Alaticaulia" but more commonly known as "Australian native ginger" or Alpinia caerulea and the closely related Alpinia cupularis  (Alaticaulia cupularis) seems to be a synonym of  Alpinia caerulea's closely related species. If this is the plant you are referring to, here is a paragraph about it:

Alpinia caerulea, also known as Australian native ginger, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alpinia caerulea include gingerols, shogaols, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
